title: Fedora 41 "Error: pod cgroup is not set: internal libpod error" with podman-compose
body:
### Issue Description

I`m trying to use podman-compose by gitlab CI/CD tool on my Linux Fedora 41 server to deploy my backend. When my image (based on python:3.12) is built i have the following errors
```
Successfully tagged localhost/<name>:latest
09c8f82215a844d9358d40337c1068208d156cc0f7a6db16a78ca4d442d0e445
Error: adding pod to state: name "pod_<name>" is in use: pod already exists
Error: pod 7e0411e7f7ab17b9274ab5310dbffbed5bde18d95faffcec80c076b05d443939 cgroup is not set: internal libpod error
``` 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Linux Fedora 41 Server
2. Podman 5.3.1
3. podman-compose up -d --build


### Describe the results you received

Errors was described above

### Describe the results you expected

I expect that all containers will run

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

When im trying to run `podman info` command by roorles user `gitlab-runner` i have the following error
```
WARN[0000] RunRoot is pointing to a path (/run/user/988/containers) which is not writable. Most likely podman will fail.
Error: default OCI runtime "crun" not found: invalid argument
```

comments.body: After I add password to my gitlab-runner user (there are no password when I create user) the issue is gone
